title: rhaphiolepis umbellata subsp. integerrima
intro: Rhaphiolepis umbellata subsp. integerrima, also known as the Evergreen Hibiscus, is a flowering plant species native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its attractive white and pink flowers and glossy green leaves. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Rhaphiolepis umbellata subsp. integerrima include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve respiratory health, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
